title: Import Docker project to Kubernetes
help: Move a Docker Compose project into the selected cluster namespace.

flow.helpStep1: Choose a source: a running Docker Compose project or your own compose file
flow.helpStep2: Generate a preview and review the resources that will be created
flow.helpStep3: Import the project into the namespace
flow.stepSourceHint: Where the service definition comes from
flow.stepPreviewHint: What will be created in Kubernetes
flow.stepImportHint: Final step: create resources in namespace {{namespace}}
flow.sourceDockerHint: Projects already running via docker compose up
flow.sourceYamlHint: Paste YAML manually or upload a file
flow.emptyDockerHint: Run docker compose up in your project folder, then refresh this page.
flow.exposePortsHint: Map host ports to NodePort (30000-32767) when possible
flow.loadImagesHint: Load images into kind if they exist only on this machine
flow.rebuildHint: Builds images with docker compose build, reapplies the compose manifest in Kubernetes, loads images into kind, updates group deployments, and restarts the whole group
flow.rebuildUnavailableDocker: Docker is unavailable in the API container. Set DOCKER_SCAN_ENABLED=true and mount docker.sock.
flow.rebuildUnavailableSource: Rebuild is only available for on-disk compose files, not for already running Docker Compose projects.
flow.previewEmpty: Click the button below to see deployments, services, and other resources.
flow.previewRequired: Generate a preview in step 3 first
flow.applyReadOnly: Import apply requires manage access to the Import section

projectsEmpty: No running Docker Compose projects found. Run docker compose up or paste custom YAML.

composeControl.title: Compose lifecycle
composeControl.hint: Stop or remove containers on the local API host.

prepare.loadFailed: Failed to load images into cluster

exposeHostPorts: Expose host ports as NodePort when possible (30000-32767)
imageOverridesHint: Tags after docker compose build, e.g. demo-shop-api:local

errors.buildNoTaggedImages: Build finished but no images were tagged for import
